message.thinking:
We need to choose a path through the coarse tree. The tree: root #1. Children: #2 and #3. #2 has child #4. #4 has children #5, #6, #7. So possible paths: 1-2-4-5, 1-2-4-6, 1-2-4-7, 1-2-4, 1-2, 1-3, 1-2-4-5-? no further. We need 2-6 posts. We want a path with useful disagreement, clarification, etc. The best path might be 1-2-4-5 or 1-2-4-6 or 1-2-4-7. Which gives good learning? Let's analyze each.

Post 1: Matt proposes standardizing custom build targets. Post 2: pf_moore questions adoption. Post 4: Matt again, seeking feedback. Post 5: bwoodsend clarifies that tools rely on setuptools historically. Post 6: steve.dower clarifies that tools are frontend tools. Post 7: cjames23 says wait until multiple tools adopt.

We want a path with disagreement, clarification, evaluation. 1-2-4-5: includes Matt's proposal, pf_moore's doubt, Matt's follow-up, bwoodsend's clarification. That includes disagreement (pf_moore doubts), clarification (bwoodsend). Good.

1-2-4-6: includes steve.dower's clarification about frontend tools. Also good.

1-2-4-7: includes cjames23's stance to wait. Also good.

Which path gives strongest reusable phrase candidates? Let's see phrase candidates:

Post 1: "I want to standardize hatchling targets." "I feel like this overcomplicates the pyproject.toml" "I'm not sure whether or not the build frontend should support building targets that are not listed in the `targets` table."

Post 2: "I don't expect pip to" "Without a frontend using these new APIs" "I don't *know* if that's the case"

Post 4: "I can't definitively say this one way of the other" "I would love to get some feedback from any of these frontend developers if this is something they can see being standardized."

Post 5: "I'm pretty certain" "only do so because they date back to when" "fully decoupled itself from setuptools"

Post 6: "What you've listed here are actually _frontend_ tools" "They aren't implemented like that, of course"

Post 7: "I want to see more than one tool implementing something with significant adoption before trying to standardize it." "many of the plugins that are not just flavors of wheels and sdists typically have relatively narrow use cases."

We want reusable source-verified phrase candidates. Many of these are good. The path 1-2-4-5 includes many. 1-2-4-6 also includes. 1-2-4-7 includes. Which path gives best flow? 1-2-4-5: after pf_moore's doubt, Matt asks for feedback, bwoodsend clarifies. Good. 1-2-4-6: after pf_moore's doubt, Matt asks for feedback, steve clarifies about frontend tools. Also good. 1-2-4-7: after pf_moore's doubt, Matt asks for feedback, cjames says wait until multiple tools adopt. Also good.

Which path has more disagreement? 1-2-4-5: pf_moore doubts, bwoodsend clarifies. 1-2-4-6: pf_moore doubts, steve clarifies. 1-2-4-7: pf_moore doubts, cjames says wait. All have disagreement.

Which path has more evaluation? 1-2-4-5: bwoodsend explains historical reasons. 1-2-4-6: steve explains frontend tools. 1-2-4-7: cjames expresses stance.

Which path has more useful phrase candidates? All have. But 1-2-4-5 includes "I'm pretty certain" and "fully decoupled itself from setuptools" which are good. 1-2-4-6 includes "What you've listed here are actually _frontend_ tools" and "They aren't implemented like that, of course". 1-2-4-7 includes "I want to see more than one tool implementing something with significant adoption before trying to standardize it." and "many of the plugins that are not just flavors of wheels and sdists typically have relatively narrow use cases."

Which path gives best conversational flow? 1-2-4-5: after pf_moore's doubt, Matt asks for feedback, bwoodsend clarifies. That seems natural. 1-2-4-6: after pf_moore's doubt, Matt asks for feedback, steve clarifies about frontend tools. Also natural. 1-2-4-7: after pf_moore's doubt, Matt asks for feedback, cjames says wait. Also natural.

We need to choose one. Let's pick 1-2-4-5 because it includes a clarification about historical reasons and independence, which is useful. Also includes "I'm pretty certain" phrase. Good.

Now we need to produce JSON with selected_path [1,2,4,5]. Path summary: something like "Matt proposes standardizing custom build targets, pf_moore questions adoption, Matt seeks feedback, bwoodsend clarifies historical reliance on setuptools and independence of tools." Provide selection reasons: mention that path includes proposal, doubt, feedback, clarification, good phrase candidates, moderate length. Rejected alternatives: we must list all other posts not selected: posts 3,6,7. Provide reason for each: e.g., 3 is unrelated to the discussion about standardization; 6 is a clarification but not as directly relevant; 7 is a stance but we already have a clarification path. Provide confidence: high.

Let's craft JSON accordingly.
